name: Webprobe Mask System
description: Mask system for suppressing known or expected security findings by matching URL patterns, title patterns, and categories against configurable rules loaded from YAML files.

types.name: MaskRule

fields.name: url_pattern
fields.description: Regex pattern matched against finding URL

fields.name: title_pattern
fields.description: Regex pattern matched against finding title

fields.name: category
fields.description: If set, must match finding category exactly

fields.name: reason
fields.description: Documentation explaining why this finding is suppressed

types.description: A single suppression rule with patterns and category matching

types.name: MaskConfig

fields.name: rules
fields.description: List of mask rules to apply

types.description: Collection of suppression rules loaded from YAML configuration

functions.name: load_mask
functions.description: Load mask configuration from a YAML file. Searches default locations if path is None. Returns empty MaskConfig if file doesn't exist or path is invalid.

inputs.name: path
inputs.description: Path to YAML mask file; None to search default locations

error_cases.name: pydantic_validation_error
error_cases.condition: YAML structure doesn't match MaskConfig schema

error_cases.name: file_read_error
error_cases.condition: File exists but cannot be read (permissions, encoding issues)

postconditions: If path is None and no default files exist, returns empty MaskConfig with empty rules list
If specified path doesn't exist, returns empty MaskConfig with empty rules list
If file exists and is valid YAML, returns MaskConfig with parsed rules

structured_side_effects.target: YAML configuration file at specified or default paths
structured_side_effects.description: Checks existence and reads content of webprobe-mask.yaml, .webprobe/mask.yaml, or specified path

functions.name: apply_mask
functions.description: Apply mask rules to a list of security findings, splitting them into kept and suppressed lists. Matches findings against rules using regex patterns for URL and title, and exact match for category.

inputs.name: findings
inputs.description: List of security findings to filter

error_cases.name: regex_error
error_cases.condition: url_pattern or title_pattern contains invalid regex syntax

error_cases.name: attribute_error
error_cases.condition: SecurityFinding missing required attributes (url, title, category)

preconditions: findings is a list (may be empty)
mask is a valid MaskConfig instance
Each finding has url (optional), title (required), and category attributes
postconditions: kept_findings + suppressed_findings contains all original findings (no loss)
No finding appears in both kept and suppressed lists (mutually exclusive)
A finding is suppressed if ANY rule matches all conditions (url_pattern AND title_pattern AND category)
If finding.url is None, url_pattern check is treated as True (match)

invariants: Default mask file search paths are ['webprobe-mask.yaml', '.webprobe/mask.yaml'] in order
MaskRule default url_pattern and title_pattern is '.*' (matches everything)
MaskRule with empty category string matches any category
apply_mask preserves all findings across (kept, suppressed) partitions
First matching rule suppresses a finding (rules are evaluated in order)

data_access.rationale: Module reads mask configuration files from filesystem, no database or network access

side_effects.rationale: Loads YAML mask rules from local configuration files

authority.rationale: This module owns the logic for loading mask rules and applying suppression patterns to security findings
